pattern: Generating every ordering is backtracking that fixes one position at a time, swapping unused elements into place and undoing the choice on the way back up.
hints: Build a permutation position by position, choosing each next element from those not yet used.
Track which indices are already placed, recurse to fill the next slot, then unmark to explore the alternative choices.
When the current arrangement has length n, record a copy; otherwise loop over unused elements, mark one used, recurse, and unmark it before trying the next.

edge_inputs.description: statement example of three distinct values
negative values must permute like any others
four distinct values give twenty-four orderings

stress.description: small permutation of distinct integers within the tight length bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, 7)
    pool = rng.sample(range(-10, 11), n)
    return [pool]
stress.note: distinct values keep the answer count exactly n! and the search small
